title: When recording first, keyboard is going to away after every letter type
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

When recording first, keyboard is going to away after every letter type

---

**Category:** `bug`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description: User reports that when recording a "first" event, the keyboard dismisses after every single keystroke, making text entry impossible. This is likely a state update or re-render issue in the recording/input component that causes the input field to lose focus on each character typed. Affected area: the "firsts" recording feature input component in src/. Expected: keyboard stays open while typing. Current: keyboard closes after each letter. Acceptance criteria: user can type full sentences without keyboard dismissing.
implementation_notes:
Files changed: src/tabs/MilestonesTab.tsx
Approach: Moved SecBody component definition outside the MilestonesTab component. When defined inside, React treats it as a new component type on every render (since the function reference changes), causing unmount/remount of the entire subtree including input fields. This caused mobile keyboards to dismiss after every keystroke. Moving it outside creates a stable reference.

title: in settings there is outdated pediatrics report
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

in settings there is outdated pediatrics report. Use the latest one which was created.

---

**Category:** `bug`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description: User reports that the pediatrics report accessible from Settings is outdated. The app should reference the latest/most recently created pediatrics report instead. Affected area: Settings page, specifically the pediatrics report link or embedded content. Expected: Settings shows the latest pediatrics report. Current: Shows an old/outdated version. Acceptance criteria: Settings page references and displays the most current pediatrics report data.
implementation_notes:
Files changed: src/features/settings/Settings.tsx, src/App.tsx
Approach: Replaced the outdated inline HTML report generator in Settings with a callback to the newer PediatrReport component (which has period selection, better formatting, vaccine tracking, and proper data aggregation). Added onShowReport prop to Settings, wired it in App.tsx to close settings and show PediatrReport modal. Removed unused imports (daysAgo, fmtTime, fmtVol, volLabel).

title: after logging in for the first time, the blank page is shown
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

after logging in for the first time, the blank page is shown. After closing the app & then opening it works

---

**Category:** `bug`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description: User reports that after logging in for the first time, a blank page is shown. The app only works correctly after closing and reopening it. This suggests a race condition or missing initialization step on first login — likely the app state or data isn't fully loaded before rendering. Affected area: authentication flow, initial app load/routing after first login. Expected: app shows the main screen immediately after first login. Current: blank page until app restart. Acceptance criteria: first-time login leads directly to a functional main screen without needing to restart the app.
implementation_notes:
Files changed: src/App.tsx
Approach: The blank page on first login was caused by logs state being initialized as {} instead of the expected structure with empty arrays. Changed initial logs state to include all categories (feed, diaper, sleep, growth, temp, bath, massage, meds, allergy) as empty arrays. Also ensured the else branch in profile data loading always sets logs to the proper structure, and both catch handlers initialize logs properly on failure. This prevents any downstream component from receiving an undefined category.

title: when you merge the feeds, if the sides are changed(breast’s), it still shows ...
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

when you merge the feeds, if the sides are changed(breast’s), it still shows risk to the later breast (if L was used initially, R is always red during that time)

---

**Category:** `bug` `feeding`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description: When merging feeds where the breast side was changed (e.g., started with L then switched to R), the risk indicator still highlights the later breast incorrectly. If L was used first, R shows as red/risk during that merged time window even though the side was switched. Affected area: feed merging logic and breast-side risk calculation in the feeding module. Expected: after merging feeds with side changes, risk indicator reflects the actual side used at each point. Acceptance criteria: merged feeds correctly track which breast was used and show risk on the correct side.
implementation_notes:
Files changed: src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx, src/features/feeding/mergeFeedSession.ts
Approach: When merging feeds with different breast sides (e.g., Breast L then Breast R), the merged feed's type field now updates to the most recent breast side. Previously, the type stayed as the original, causing risk indicators to show danger on the wrong breast. Added isBreastSwitch detection that only applies when both the old and new types are breast feeds. The original side info is preserved in the notes field.

title: the caurasel is good but how does someone know that scroll is needed, maybe s...
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

the caurasel is good but how does someone know that scroll is needed, maybe show 1 & .25 of the next one?

---

**Category:** `bug`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description: User reports that the carousel has no visual affordance indicating that scrolling is possible. Suggestion: show 1 full card and approximately 0.25 of the next card to hint at scrollable content. Affected area: carousel component on the dashboard/home screen. Expected: carousel visually indicates more content is available by partially showing the next item. Current: no scroll hint, user may not realize they can swipe. Acceptance criteria: carousel shows a partial peek of the next card to indicate scrollability.
implementation_notes:
Files changed: src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx
Approach: Changed carousel from showing one slide at a time to showing the current slide at 88% width with a visible peek (12%) of the next slide. Used CSS flexbox with overflow:hidden and translateX transform for smooth sliding. Non-active slides are shown at 50% opacity to visually hint that they are swipeable. This provides clear scroll affordance.
